Enzyme_Name.reason: Paper calls it cinnamyl alcohol dehydrogenase 7 and concludes it functions as a small-molecule aldehyde reductase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CAD family and MDR superfamily are stated; NADPH dependence is experimentally shown.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: AtCAD7 was cloned from Col-0 cDNA.

Applied_Species.reason: AtCAD7 functional assays in planta used Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: WT and transformants are in Col-0 background.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Results link AtCAD7 to histamine metabolic/catabolic processing and also to altered phenylpropanoid metabolism in silenced lines.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Figure 4b and text specifically propose reduction of histamine-derived aldehyde intermediates.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Paper presents AtCAD7 as acting on histamine-derived aldehyde intermediates in catabolic processing that affects histamine accumulation, not as the biosynthetic decarboxylase step.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Paper does not claim rate-limiting status for AtCAD7, but supports it as a control point affecting histamine accumulation through depletion of aldehyde intermediates and feedback on HDC.

Primary_Substrate.reason: All listed aldehydes were tested as substrates except that imidazole-4-carbaldehyde and 1-methylimidazole-4-carboxaldehyde are analogs of the native histamine-derived intermediates, not proven native substrates.

Primary_Product.reason: AtCAD7 is shown to reduce aldehydes to corresponding alcohols; TLC confirms alcohol formation for representative substrates, and Figure 4b proposes corresponding alcohols for histamine-derived aldehydes.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: NADPH-dependent reduction of diverse small aldehydes is directly shown; for histamine pathway, the tested compounds are structural analogs of the intermediates.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Figure 4b and text explicitly propose that reducing aldehyde intermediates relieves inhibition on HDC and increases histamine accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Histamine is the final metabolite connected to AtCAD7 overexpression.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: This class description is chemically reasonable but not explicitly provided in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Histamine was quantified from leaf samples.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Figure 4e demonstrates histamine promotes susceptibility to P. parasitica.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These factors were all directly used in the study.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results support divergence from AtCAD5, very low/negligible activity toward p-coumaraldehyde, and similarity in substrate-binding context to bacterial YahK/YjgB.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are described and used.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Overexpression increased histamine accumulation and susceptibility in Arabidopsis.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results support weakened immunity with elevated histamine and shift toward defense phenylpropanoids/flavonoids upon silencing.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Summary matches Results, with the caveat that histamine-derived aldehydes tested were structural analogs, so native substrate aspect is indirect.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Consistent with Abstract and Discussion about CAD7 as target for enhancing resistance.

Omics_Data.reason: UPLC-MS/MS metabolomics and data availability statement are explicit.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Text explicitly refers to histidine decarboxylase (HDC) and NbHDC constructs.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number 4.1.1.22 appears in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Discussion states HDC is PLP-dependent in plants generally, but NbHDC itself is not explicitly annotated as a PLP-dependent amino acid decarboxylase in Results/Methods.

Source_Species.reason: NbHDC was amplified from Nicotiana benthamiana cDNA.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional assays for NbHDC were performed in N. benthamiana.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Paper states HDC is rate-limiting in histamine biosynthesis pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: HDC catalyses conversion of histidine to histamine, i.e. histidine decarboxylation step.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Results explicitly call HDC the rate-limiting enzyme, supporting committed/rate-limiting role.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Text says HDC catalyses conversion of histidine to histamine.

Primary_Product.reason: Histamine is explicitly the product of HDC-catalysed reaction.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Paper directly states HDC catalyses conversion of histidine to histamine.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: HDC directly forms histamine according to the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: NbHDC is connected to histamine accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The class 'amino acid-derived amine' is not explicitly stated, though chemically inferable.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Transient expression and histamine measurements were in N. benthamiana leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Overexpression of GFP-NbHDC increased susceptibility to P. parasitica in the experimental system.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Methods/Results describe transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: NbHDC assays involved treatment with imidazole-4-carbaldehyde and 1-methylimidazole-4-carboxaldehyde and infection after transient expression.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Supported by Results: NbHDC used for mechanistic validation, overexpression raised histamine and susceptibility, and aldehyde intermediates lowered NbHDC protein levels.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly described: transient overexpression, LC-MS/MS, infection assay, ELISA-based HDC detection.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Figure S6 shows NbHDC overexpression increased histamine and susceptibility to P. parasitica.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Higher histamine correlating with greater disease susceptibility is directly shown.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are explicitly supported by Results and Figure S6.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable inference, but the paper does not present NbHDC as an engineering target; the main proposed target is CAD7.

Omics_Data.reason: Targeted LC-MS/MS histamine quantification was used.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Paper states pART27-N vector and transient overexpression, but does not specify a 35S-like promoter.

corrections.reason: No EC number 4.1.1.22 appears in the paper.

corrections.old_value: 35S-like transient overexpression context via agroinfiltration in pART27-N system
